# Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview

This repository uses **GitHub Agentic Workflows (gh-aw)** to automate a weekly digest of GitHub Copilot and AI coding developments. Workflows are defined as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter and compiled to GitHub Actions lock files.

## Critical Rules

- **Never edit `.lock.yml` files directly.** They are auto-generated by `gh aw compile` from the corresponding `.md` source files. The same applies to `agentics-maintenance.yml`.
- **Never edit `.github/aw/actions-lock.json` directly.** It is managed by `gh aw compile`.
- Always edit the `.md` workflow source in `.github/workflows/`, then recompile.

## Workflow Authoring

- Workflow source files live at `.github/workflows/*.md` and use YAML frontmatter for configuration (triggers, permissions, engine, tools, safe-outputs, network allowlist).
- The execution engine is `claude-opus-4` via the Anthropic API.
- Use `safe-outputs` for any elevated operations (creating/updating discussions, issues, PRs). Never bypass safe-outputs with raw API calls.
- Keep the `network.allowed` list minimal — only add domains the workflow actually needs to reach.

## Compiling & Validation

- Compile workflows: `gh aw compile`
- Validate without writing: `gh aw compile --validate`
- Fix Dependabot PRs: `gh aw compile --dependabot` (never merge Dependabot PRs that touch generated manifests directly)

## Version Pinning

- Always pin `gh-aw` to an explicit version in `copilot-setup-steps.yml` (both the action ref `github/gh-aw/actions/setup-cli@vX.Y.Z` and the `version` input must match).
- When upgrading, update both values in lockstep and recompile all workflows with `gh aw compile`.
